demo.description:
My previous projects mainly involved B2B services and system development that wasn't publicly available, so I don't have any publicly available work to share.
Therefore, I'm sharing demos using publicly available APIs and systems I developed personally.

poke.description:
This is a demo that uses the Pokemon API and is styled like a Pokemon card.
When you enter the Pokedex number or name, it displays information such as the image, height, weight, and type of that Pokemon.
Please be careful as pressing the speaker icon will play a sound.
poke.input_favorite_pokemon: Enter the Pokedex number or name of your favorite Pokemon
poke.pickup: Don't know much? You can choose and enter it here.

three.description:
A demo that creates a 3D model with Tinker Cad and displays it from any angle with Three.js.
It's still a while away from completion because creating the 3D model takes time, but it will eventually look like a jungle.

sabbatical.description:
This is a sabbatical management app.
I will continue to improve the functionality in the future.
You need to register your email address to use it, so an image is posted.
sabbatical.tech: Tech stack: React, TypeScript, Next.js, Supabase, Vercel

demo.nasa_text: API to retrieve "Todays Astronomy Picture of the Day"
demo.fox_text: It is a healing API that only retrieves a random fox image

list.1:
Originally from Fukuoka.
After graduating from school, I joined a major telecommunications company.
list.2:
I wanted to develop my skills, so I attended a web design school that interested me.
Later, I worked at a television station and consulting company, handling direction, design, and coding.
list.3: I became interested in the wider world and studied in Canada for a year.
list.4: As I had more opportunities to work with CMS and jQuery, I wanted to expand my programming skills, so I studied Java and PHP at a school and joined a systems development company.
list.5: After several years, I became a freelancer.

list.3: Living things, nature, architecture, art...so many things👀

list.2: Prefers simplicity and clarity; dislikes meaningless conventions or wasted effort.

list.1:
The recent progress in AI has been remarkable.
I enjoy talking with it every day, almost like chatting with a knowledgeable friend.
list.2: And perhaps, with AI, we'll enter an era where system development can be done without programmers.
list.3: So, what can't AI do? Wouldn't it be better if humans evolved to compensate for those shortcomings?
list.4: What AI struggles with is understanding 'human feelings and emotions that are difficult to put into words' and 'different cultural backgrounds.' I believe that the feel and awkwardness of UI and UX, which can only be understood through human eyes and senses, ultimately have to be adjusted by humans.
list.5: Previously, I aspired to be a full-stack engineer. However, I believe that the backend, which is easier to articulate and invisible to humans, is more of an area where AI excels and is likely to be replaced in the future.
list.6: My future goal is to 'develop better things by complementing each other's weaknesses with AI and combining our strengths.' To that end, I'm learning more about AI while honing my front-end skills.

contact.message: To contact me, please copy my email address from the button below
